Germany Lab
Formal Questions and Answers

This document records the validated answers for the Germany lab challenge in a clear and formal question-and-answer format.

Q1. What is the MITRE ATT&CK ID for the delivery of the malicious file?
Answer: T1566.001

Q2. What is the MD5 hash of the malicious file?
Answer: 5797120155f72b86920a708ab2ec607c

Q3. What is the full reconstructed command string?
Answer: powershell -NoProfile -WindowStyle Hidden -Command [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetString([System.Convert]::FromBase64String('')) | Invoke-Expression

Q4. Identify the specific Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) namespace string used by the script to interface with the system management layer and execute the payload.
Answer: winmgmts:root\cimv2

Q5. What is the URL of the second-stage?
Answer: http://archve.org/MSI_PRO_20281135.png

Q6. What is the MD5 hash of the second-stage?
Answer: 569e616d712de880288c6197c01539bd

Q7. What is the MD5 hash of the extracted payload?
Answer: e9c3acfd36278fb891d4f44c15a68bb2

Q8. Identifying the legitimate origin of the extracted payload: what is the GitHub repository URL associated with this library?
Answer: https://github.com/dahall/TaskScheduler

Q9. What is the MITRE ATT&CK technique used for loading the payload?
Answer: T1620

Q10. What is the name of the function invoked by the loader?
Answer: VAi

Q11. What is the full Registry key path for persistence?
Answer: HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run

Q12. What is the Registry value name for persistence?
Answer: displayable

Q13. What is the command string stored in the Registry value?
Answer: wscript.exe //b "C:\Users\Public\Downloads\K7vX2m9L.js"

Q14. Which anti-VM techniques are implemented in the loader?
Answer:
- Querying BIOS and System Manufacturer information
- Checking for hardware resource limitations
- Verifying the presence of specific registry keys and ACPI tables

Q15. What is the final payload download URL?
Answer: http://cutec.co.za/arquivo_20211526075950.txt

Q16. Once the final payload is downloaded, what is the full file system path where it is stored on the infected host?
Answer: C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\Temp\D4jN6p1R.exe

Q17. What is the MD5 hash of the final Remcos payload?
Answer: db9adcb66be33e264f7cbaeda8d2c3f9

Q18. What is the Type and Name of the resource where the C2 configuration is stored? (Format: TYPE:NAME)
Answer: RCDATA:SETTINGS

Q19. Which algorithm is used to encrypt the configuration?
Answer: RC4

Q20. What is the decryption key in hex?
Answer: 1df356165637c3b6064086180f2d55d8

Q21. What are the magic bytes that represent the Remcos signature?
Answer: 2404ff00

Q22. What is this exact hex sequence used as delimiter?
Answer: 7c1e1e1f7c

Q23. What is the exact Mutex string name used by this bot instance to identify its active instance?
Answer: Global\Rmc-ID3RBF

Q24. What is the MITRE ATT&CK technique ID for the specific type of Process Injection used by the watchdog?
Answer: T1055.012

Q25. What is the full file path where the watchdog process is injected?
Answer: C:\Windows\System32\fsutil.exe

Q26. What is the amount of time (in seconds) that the malware remains silent before initiating C2 communication?
Answer: 7

Q27. What is the full "Hex:Command" pattern sent for the first execution involving the Windows CLI utility?
Answer: 0e:netstat -ano

Q28. Question and validated answer were not supplied in the provided lab notes.
Answer: Not provided in source material.

Q29. What is the full file system path of the exfiltrated file?
Answer: C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Roaming\Screenshots\time_20260326_161322.bmp

Q30. What is the MD5 hash of the exfiltrated file?
Answer: 9ba02a9132294d54bd7f137175c14a7b

Q31. What is the real name of the third-party binary module executed for credential harvesting?
Answer: WebBrowserPassView.exe

Q32. What is the full command executed by the malware to steal passwords?
Answer: "C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\Temp\frAQBc.exe" /stext "C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\Temp\frAQBc.txt"

Q33. What are the specific credentials stolen by the malware during its harvesting phase? (email:password)
Answer: cicamo3022@muncloud.com:p@ssw0rd9753

Q34. The attacker also utilized an online keylogger during the session. What specific string was captured?
Answer: secdojo[SHIFT]@9628

Q35. What is the URL of the external API used by the malware to identify its geographical location?
Answer: http://geoplugin.net/json.gp

Q36. What is the Windows API function called when the attacker sends command 0x26?
Answer: MessageBoxA

Q37. What is the raw string sent by the attacker when using command 0x26?
Answer: Secdojo|you have been hacked

Q38. What is the full command executed to achieve self-deletion and cleanup?
Answer: wscript.exe "C:\Users\ADMINI~1\AppData\Local\Temp\8Wsa1xVPfv.vbs"
